commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh 2 Chronicles 24:3 And Jehoiada took for him two wives; and he begat sons and daughters. Joas, as Protestants (Haydock) and most interpreters understand it, with the Syriac Joiada was too old to think of marrying again, as the Rabbins would explain the Hebrew, conformably to the Septuagint and Arabic, "himself. "(Calmet) Joas did not probably marry two at once. (Salien, the year of the world 3766. ) Amasias was born of Joadan, chap, xxv. (Menochius) The high priest considered Joas as his son, being married to his aunt.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
